html:
The Lazy Forager is a craftable item found in the Ancient Tab. It requires 1 Orange Gem, 2 Thulecite, and 3 Nightmare Fuel to craft at a repaired Ancient Pseudoscience Station to prototype. It also can randomly drop from an Ancient Pseudoscience Station when the latter is damaged with a Hammer, Gunpowder etc.

While having it equipped in the chest slot, it allows the player to automatically pick up inventory items that lie on the ground, without having to interact with them. The items will disappear from the ground with a puff of smoke and then appear in the inventory. The Lazy Forager has a limited amount of uses and after it has collected 225 items it will break. It will also provide the player with a passive +2 per minute. The Lazy Forager can be refueled with Nightmare Fuel, restoring 22% Durability.

Downloadable Content

In the Reign of Giants DLC, players have a 0.02% chance of finding Orange Gems inside Tumbleweeds, thus making Lazy Foragers renewable.

As of the mega update, orange gems have become renewable with the Seed of Ruin allowing the player to regenerate the ruins to acquire more orange gems.


Tips

  • The Lazy Forager can be used to pick up the rare items from Cave Holes. New treasures spawn in the Cave Holes every time the Ruins are reset.
  • The Lazy Forager can be used to collect Salt Crystals that drop out of pickup range near mined Salt Formations. This can make it less tedious to manoeuver around the mined Salt Formations each time the player wants to collect the Salt Crystals.
    • Similarly, any items floating in the ocean out of the players reach can be retrieved with The Lazy Forager.
  • During the Pig King Wrestling Match in DST, the Lazy Forager will automatically pick up any Gold Nuggets, whether dropped by the Pig King or by the player himself.
  • Despite being refuellable, The Lazy Forager will still break at 0%, so care must be taken.
  • The Lazy Forager will not count full stacks as one item when reducing durability - instead, it will pull one piece out of the stack at a time. It will also pick up any Traps, Tooth Traps, or Bee Mines nearby.
  • The Lazy Forager can be used to aid in clearing out landspace to expand the player's base. It will automatically pick up any Turfs, Logs, Saplings, Rocks and other resources that are dropped when clearing out various naturally spawned obstacles.

Trivia

  • The Lazy Forager shares the same in-game model with the Chilled Amulet but its gem is orange instead of blue.
  • This item used to be a part of the Gemology Tab before the Tab was removed from the game.

Assets

  • Dropped
  • Equipped

Gallery

  • Wilson wearing The Lazy Forager.
  • Willow wearing The Lazy Forager.
  • Wolfgang wearing The Lazy Forager.
  • Wendy wearing The Lazy Forager.
  • WX-78 wearing The Lazy Forager.
  • Wickerbottom wearing The Lazy Forager.
  • Woodie wearing The Lazy Forager.
  • Wes wearing The Lazy Forager.
  • Maxwell wearing The Lazy Forager.
  • Wigfrid wearing The Lazy Forager.
  • Webber wearing The Lazy Forager.
  • Walani wearing The Lazy Forager.
  • Warly wearing The Lazy Forager.
  • Wilbur wearing The Lazy Forager.
  • Woodlegs wearing The Lazy Forager.
  • Wilba wearing The Lazy Forager.
  • Wormwood wearing The Lazy Forager.
  • Wheeler wearing The Lazy Forager.
  • Winona wearing The Lazy Forager.
  • Wortox wearing The Lazy Forager.
  • Wurt wearing The Lazy Forager.

–Wortox
“It make me feel fancy, flurp.”
–Wurt
“Getting there is half the fun!”
–Walter
“I can save so much time if I don't have to stop and pick things up!”
–Wanda
The Lazy Forager is a craftable item found in the Ancient Tab. It requires 1 Orange Gem, 2 Thulecite, and 3 Nightmare Fuel to craft at a repaired Ancient Pseudoscience Station to prototype. It also can randomly drop from an Ancient Pseudoscience Station when the latter is damaged with a Hammer, Gunpowder etc.
While having it equipped in the chest slot, it allows the player to automatically pick up inventory items that lie on the ground, without having to interact with them. The items will disappear from the ground with a puff of smoke and then appear in the inventory. The Lazy Forager has a limited amount of uses and after it has collected 225 items it will break. It will also provide the player with a passive +2 per minute. The Lazy Forager can be refueled with Nightmare Fuel, restoring 22% Durability.
Downloadable Content[edit | edit source]
In the Reign of Giants DLC, players have a 0.02% chance of finding Orange Gems inside Tumbleweeds, thus making Lazy Foragers renewable.
As of the mega update, orange gems have become renewable with the Seed of Ruin allowing the player to regenerate the ruins to acquire more orange gems.
Tips[edit | edit source]
The Lazy Forager can be used to pick up the rare items from Cave Holes. New treasures spawn in the Cave Holes every time the Ruins are reset.
The Lazy Forager can be used to collect Salt Crystals that drop out of pickup range near mined Salt Formations. This can make it less tedious to manoeuver around the mined Salt Formations each time the player wants to collect the Salt Crystals.
Similarly, any items floating in the ocean out of the players reach can be retrieved with The Lazy Forager.
During the Pig King Wrestling Match in DST, the Lazy Forager will automatically pick up any Gold Nuggets, whether dropped by the Pig King or by the player himself.
Despite being refuellable, The Lazy Forager will still break at 0%, so care must be taken.
The Lazy Forager will not count full stacks as one item when reducing durability - instead, it will pull one piece out of the stack at a time. It will also pick up any Traps, Tooth Traps, or Bee Mines nearby.
The Lazy Forager can be used to aid in clearing out landspace to expand the player's base. It will automatically pick up any Turfs, Logs, Saplings, Rocks and other resources that are dropped when clearing out various naturally spawned obstacles.

The Lazy Forager shares the same in-game model with the Chilled Amulet but its gem is orange instead of blue.
This item used to be a part of the Gemology Tab before the Tab was removed from the game.

summaryViHtml:
Tóm tắt

Máy Kiếm Ăn Lười Biếng là một vật phẩm có thể chế tạo được tìm thấy trong Thẻ Cổ. Nó cần 1 Đá quý Cam, 2 Thulecite và 3 Nhiên liệu Ác mộng để chế tạo tại Trạm Giả khoa học Cổ đại đã được sửa chữa thành nguyên mẫu. Nó cũng có thể rơi ngẫu nhiên từ Trạm Giả Khoa Cổ Đại khi Trạm Giả Khoa Cổ Đại bị hư hại bởi Búa, Thuốc Súng, v.v.
